title: Energizing Floral Silky Kenyan Barramundi Stir-fry
description: A floral and silky Kenyan-inspired dish featuring barramundi with a tandoor twist for a pescatarian-friendly meal, bursting with energy.
instructions: Heat oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat.
Add sliced ginger, Thai chili peppers, and garlic. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Throw in the turmeric powder and cumin seeds; continue to stir for another minute.
Place the barramundi fillets into the wok. Coat evenly with a mixture of cornstarch and soy sauce (if using).
Pour coconut milk into the wok, ensuring the fish is well coated by the liquid.
Bring the stir-fry to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for about 10-15 minutes until all liquids are absorbed and the fish is cooked through.
Stir in leaves of rosemary and thyme. Serve hot as your energizing floral silken Kenyan Barramundi Stir-Fry.
